title: Adobe OAuth credentials should not be disclosed
description: Adobe OAuth credentials should not be hardcoded in source code. These credentials include Adobe client IDs, client secrets, and access tokens that provide access to Adobe Creative Cloud, Analytics, Experience Platform and other Adobe services. When exposed in code repositories, they can lead to unauthorized access to Adobe services and user data.
recommendation: Store Adobe OAuth credentials securely using environment variables, secure configuration management, or Adobe's recommended secure storage practices. Never commit client secrets or access tokens directly to source code repositories.
impact: Critical security risk - unauthorized access to Adobe services, Creative Cloud accounts, and user data

compliant.description: Using environment variable for Adobe client ID

compliant.code:
# Use secure configuration management
client_secret = config.get_secret('adobe_oauth_secret')

checks.message: Adobe OAuth client ID detected in source code
Adobe OAuth client secret (UUID format) detected in source code
Adobe JWT access token detected in source code
Potential Adobe OAuth credential detected in source code
